name: New Orleans Silver And Gold
description_short: New Orleans Silver and Gold Exchange buys and sells precious metals, jewelry, coins, and bullion using live spot pricing. They offer free appraisals and mobile buying services across Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, and Florida.
description_long:
New Orleans Silver and Gold Exchange operates as a precious metals buyer and seller specializing in gold, silver, and rare coins. Founded to serve the New Orleans area, the company has expanded its service territory across multiple states including Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, and Florida, covering parishes from Jefferson to Plaquemines in Louisiana and cities as far as Jackson, Mississippi and Pensacola, Florida. The company positions itself as an alternative to traditional pawn shops, focusing exclusively on precious metals rather than general merchandise.

The business offers comprehensive precious metals services including buying and selling used or broken jewelry, silver and gold coins, bullion bars, sterling silver flatware, rare numismatic coins, and loose gems. They provide free appraisals using live spot pricing to determine current market value based on international gold and silver prices. The company operates a physical location at 935 Gravier Street in New Orleans and advertises mobile buying services, bringing specialists directly to clients.

What distinguishes New Orleans Silver and Gold is their emphasis on transparency and customer service. Their website highlights a staff member named Alex who appears repeatedly in recent reviews (dated March-September 2026), suggesting strong customer relationships and consistency. The company advertises their expertise in live spot pricing, professional testing, and fair market valuations. Customer reviews emphasize knowledgeable staff, accommodating service, and a professional environment. The company specifically markets itself as "Louisiana's best mobile silver and gold buyer."

However, the business model creates inherent limitations. As a precious metals buyer, they are fundamentally transaction-based rather than providing ongoing financial services or credit products. Their service quality appears dependent on individual staff members (particularly the frequently-mentioned Alex), raising questions about consistency if key personnel change. The website lacks detailed pricing transparency, fee structures, or buyback percentages despite mentioning "average payout percentage." For consumers unfamiliar with precious metals markets, reliance on the company's assessment of "fair" pricing requires trust rather than market verification.
pros: Free appraisals with no obligation to sell
Live spot pricing ensures valuations reflect current market rates for gold and silver
Mobile buying service brings specialists to clients across multiple states
Extensive geographic service area covering Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, and Florida
Specializes exclusively in precious metals rather than general pawn merchandise
Multiple customer reviews (8+ recent reviews) confirm consistent knowledgeable and friendly service
Transparent testing process—customers report items are tested for free
cons: No published pricing structure or standard buyback percentages; customers must visit in person to learn specific offers
Service quality appears heavily dependent on individual staff member (Alex) mentioned in majority of reviews, creating potential consistency issues
Website does not disclose accepted item conditions, minimum lot sizes, or purchase limitations
Mobile service availability and scheduling process not clearly explained on website
best_for: People with inherited jewelry, coins, or silver flatware they want to convert to cash quickly
Collectors of rare or numismatic coins seeking accurate valuations from specialists
Individuals in Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, or Florida with precious metals to sell who prefer professional evaluation over online estimates
diagnosis: New Orleans Silver and Gold is best suited for people with jewelry, coins, or bullion to liquidate who want a professional valuation with live market pricing. The main caveat is that this is a one-time transaction service, not a financial product—it does not provide credit, loans, or ongoing financial management. They are fundamentally a precious metals dealer, not a credit or lending service.
services: Buy and sell used or broken jewelry
Buy and sell silver coins, bars, and bullion
Buy and sell gold coins, bars, and bullion
Buy and sell sterling silver flatware
Buy and sell rare numismatic coins
Buy and sell loose gems and diamonds
In-store transactions at 935 Gravier Street, New Orleans
Mobile precious metals buying service (house calls)
